TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_farming_Muu

S.FORM: sua makasin ia te cu ku una kari poisua.
S.TRANSL: 現在,我有話要說。
Now, I have something to say.

S.FORM: mehua ia matirupang cu ikita ʉ taiuan isi acei cu ngʉco lipʉn.
S.TRANSL: 我們在台灣,完成了,日本就走了。
We were in Taiwan. It was finished. Japan had gone.

S.FORM: makasua ci ia sua nguco ra’ʉvʉ maku ia mʉmʉkʉ cu tammi tanʉkʉ tʉvʉsʉ mata tangtang.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣,我的太太呢, 種地瓜、芋頭、甘蔗和南瓜。
Then, my wife started planting sweet potatoes, taros, sugar canes, and pumpkins.

S.FORM: kavang nimʉmʉkʉ kavang mehua nenen vanai ta makasua? mumumuku ngʉco sua nononoman manasi ’an kia tia mooravu.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼我們要這樣種東西，因為以後不會餓。
Why we planted things this way? It is because we wouldn't get hungry.

S.FORM: mesua ia vo’ura pa ’ʉnai.
S.TRANSL: 那時候田還新的。
At that time, fields were still new.

S.FORM: noo akeen ngʉco sua akeen tia kʉna manu ia 'una seritung una navatʉ una tʉvʉsʉ.
S.TRANSL: 如果小朋友沒有吃東西的時候，還好有木瓜、芭樂，和甘蔗。
If kids had nothing to eat, thankfully there were papayas, guavas, and sugarcane.

S.FORM: makasua cu po’ocipin ngʉco rʉ’ʉvʉ maku tammi mata tanʉkʉ.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣，我的太太煮地瓜和芋頭。
My wife cooked sweet potatoes and taros.

S.FORM: noo macuru ci ia aricaan ci kee na na toomarʉ masii maraa mara mataturicang.
S.TRANSL: 她撈起(地瓜)放在簸箕裡，使比較涼。
When they were well-cooked, she scooped up and put them on a scoop to cool them.

S.FORM: mehua ia cucuru miarinasʉ kia akia nupasʉ suai mehua ia.
S.TRANSL: 那時，我非常困苦，沒有錢財。
At that time, my life was really hard. I didn't have much money.

S.FORM: makasua cu una cu arane na cau karanana nimavici pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣，有了從外地來的人攜帶了稻米。
Then, some people outside world brought rice in.

S.FORM: tavarʉ’ʉn cumancu.
S.TRANSL: (稻子)可以被插秧。
Rice seedlings can be transplanted.

S.FORM: nukai iname ia tia kovangvangʉn ma tapuroo sua ngʉco pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 若不驅趕過鳥，稻米就只有被麻雀吃光光。
If we did not drive them away, all the rice would be eaten out.

S.FORM: mon cu nia mamarurang minʉna makasi na noo aratanavikarʉ ci a pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 老人家在那邊驅趕鳥，一直到稻田變成黃色的時候。
The elders drove birds away there until the color of the fields turned yellow.

S.FORM: nakai paripusiamʉn akia ta ngʉco tia tasʉ’a nia.
S.TRANSL: 但是，收割稻米之後，卻沒有存放它們的場所。
However, having harvested them, we did not have the places to store.

S.FORM: nuu parin ci ia nuu maringunaa ci ia m m una ngʉco pangi una ngʉco tarʉkʉra mon cu nia mioru nukai tassa ia taturu.
S.TRANSL: 在曝曬過之後，那邊有杵和臼，兩位到三位會在那邊舂搗。
After drying them, there were pestle and mortar. Two or three people would pound the rice there.

S.FORM: ioruun cu kee makasi na akia tʉʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他們會舂稻穀直到沒有稻殼。
They would pound until husks disappeared.

S.FORM: nuu makasua ci ia patakoruun cu vura ihua umʉsʉ’ʉ na ’uia.
S.TRANSL: 那樣之後，就把那個米撈出來，放在鐵製的容器。
After that, rice will be scooped out and placed in iron containers.

S.FORM: siminsin po’ocipi.
S.TRANSL: 會被拿去沖洗和蒸煮。
the rice will be washed and cooked.

S.FORM: nakai sua mamarurang miana ia tavarʉ’ʉ una kunamʉ una apasʉ una arasakai.
S.TRANSL: 以前的老人家知道有川苔、螃蟹、蝦子。
Elders in the past knew there were moss, crabs and shrimps.

S.FORM: ihua ia camai miana ngʉco ihua ngʉco sua tia siakʉn ngʉco uru.
S.TRANSL: 這些川苔螃蟹蝦子就用來配飯。
These moss, crabs, and shrimps would be taken to be garnishes.
